name: Red Panda
summary: Red pandas are small, tree-climbing mammals of the Himalayan mountains. They eat mostly bamboo, use a thumb-like wrist bone to grip stems, and rest in cool forest trees.
metaTitle: Red Panda Facts for Kids | Habitat, Diet, Behavior & Photos
metaDescription: Discover red panda facts for kids with photos, Himalayan habitat, bamboo diet, tree-climbing behavior, and conservation. Learn where red pandas live and how they survive.
speed: Slow on ground; agile climber in trees
funFacts: Red pandas are not closely related to giant pandas—they belong to their own family, Ailuridae.
A red panda's long, ringed tail helps it balance on branches high in the forest.
Red pandas have a special wrist bone that works like a thumb for holding bamboo.
Red pandas were once called "firefox" because of their reddish fur and bushy tail.
A red panda can eat up to one third of its body weight in bamboo every day.
Red pandas are most active at dawn and dusk, when they search for food.

faq.question: What is a red panda?
faq.answer: A red panda is a small tree-climbing mammal from the Himalayas. It has reddish fur, a ringed tail, and eats mostly bamboo.

faq.question: Is a red panda a bear?
faq.answer: No. Red pandas are not bears and are not closely related to giant pandas. They belong to their own family called Ailuridae.

faq.question: Where do red pandas live?
faq.answer: Red pandas live in cool mountain forests in Nepal, India, Bhutan, China, and Myanmar. They need bamboo and tall trees.

faq.question: What do red pandas eat?
faq.answer: Red pandas eat mostly bamboo leaves and shoots. They also sometimes eat fruit, berries, eggs, and insects.

faq.question: Are red pandas endangered?
faq.answer: Yes. Red pandas are listed as Endangered because their forest homes are shrinking and they face hunting and habitat loss.

faq.question: Do red pandas live in groups?
faq.answer: Adult red pandas usually live alone. Mothers raise cubs until the young pandas can survive on their own.
